name: pygreat

metadata.name: pygreat
metadata.summary: Python library for talking with libGreat devices

documentation:
# libgreat host-side libraries

This folder contains an experimental host-side python utility. It should work with
both python2 and python3.

modules.name: pygreat.errors
documentation: This file is part of GreatFET

functions.name: pygreat.errors.get_error_name
documentation: Returns the error string for the given libgreat name, or None if none exists

functions.name: pygreat.errors.from_greatfet_error
documentation: Returns the error class appropriate for the given GreatFET error.

classes.name: pygreat.errors.DeviceNotFoundError
documentation: Error indicating no GreatFET device was found.

classes.name: pygreat.errors.DeviceBusyError
documentation: Error indicating the GreatFET is too busy to service the given request.

classes.name: pygreat.errors.DeviceMemoryError
documentation: Error indicating that the GreatFET has run out of memory.

classes.name: pygreat.errors.NotFoundError
documentation: Error indicating that a resource was not found.

classes.name: pygreat.errors.GreatFETError
documentation: Runtime error used when no better description is available.

classes.name: pygreat.errors.ExternalDeviceError
documentation:
Error used when a external device (e.g. not on the GreatFET)
experiences an issue. This typically means that the error is not wit
the GreatFET hardware or software, but may be with e.g. connections.

modules.name: pygreat.board
documentation: Module containing the core definitions for a libgreat-driven board.
This file is part of libgreat

functions.name: pygreat.board._to_hex_string
documentation: Convert a byte array to a hex string.

classes.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.autodetect
documentation:
Attempts to create a new instance of the GreatBoard subclass
most applicable to the given device. For example, if the attached
board is a GreatFET One, this will automatically create a
GreatFET One object.

Accepts the same arguments as pyusb's usb.find() method, allowing narrowing
to a more specific GreatFET by e.g. serial number.

Throws a DeviceNotFoundError if no device is avaiable.

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.autodetect_all
documentation:
Attempts to create a new instance of the GreatBoard subclass
most applicable for each board present on the system-- similar to the
behavior of autodetect.

Accepts the same arguments as pyusb's usb.find() method, allowing narrowing
to a more specific GreatFET by e.g. serial number.

Returns a list of GreatFET devices, which may be empty if none are found.

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.all_accepted_devices
documentation:
Returns a list of all devices supported by the given class. This should be
overridden if the device connects via anything other that USB.

Accepts the same arguments as pyusb's usb.find() method, allowing narrowing
to a more specific GreatFET by e.g. serial number.

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.accepts_connected_device
documentation:
Returns true iff the provided class is appropriate for handling a connected
GreatFET.

Accepts the same arguments as pyusb's usb.find() method, allowing narrowing
to a more specific GreatFET by e.g. serial number.

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.populate_default_identifiers
documentation:
Populate a dictionary of default identifiers-- which can
be overridden or extended by arguments to the function.

device_identifiers -- any user-specified identifers; will override
    the default identifiers in the event of a conflit

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.__init__
documentation:
Instantiates a new connection to a libgreat device; by default connects
to the first available board.

Accepts the same arguments as pyusb's usb.find() method, allowing narrowing
to a more specific board by serial number.

FIXME: accept an identifier string or a comms_backend object instead of the
array above

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.version_warnings
documentation:
Returns any warning messages relevant to the device's firmware version.
Can be used to warn the user when an upgrade is required.

Returns a string with any warnings, or None  if no warnings apply.

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.initialize_apis
documentation:
Hook-point for sub-boards to initialize their APIs after
we have comms up and running and auto-enumeration is complete.

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.supports_api
documentation: Returns true iff the board supports the given API class.

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.board_id
documentation: Reads the board ID number for the device.

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.board_name
documentation: Returns the human-readable product-name for the device.

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.firmware_version
documentation: Reads the board's firmware version.

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.serial_number
documentation: Reads the board's unique serial number.

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.part_id
documentation: Reads the board's unique serial number.

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.try_reconnect
documentation: Attempts to re-create a connection to a disconnected GreatFET.

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.reset
documentation:
Reset the device.

Arguments:
    reconect -- If True, this method will wait for the device to
        finish the reset and then attempt to reconnect.
    switch_to_external_clock -- If true, the device will accept a 12MHz
        clock signal on P4_7 (J2_P11 on the GreatFET one) after the reset.
    is_post_firmware_flash -- If true, the device will be notified that this immediately
        follows a firmware flash, and can adjust its internal messages accordingly.

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.switch_to_external_clock
documentation:
Resets the GreatFET, and starts it up again using an external clock
source, rather than the onboard crystal oscillator.

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.close
documentation:
Dispose resources allocated by this connection.  This connection
will no longer be usable.

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.read_debug_ring
documentation:
Requests the GreatFET's debug ring.

Args:
    max_length -- The maximum length to respond with. Must be less than 65536.
    clear -- True iff the dmesg buffer should be cleared after the request.

methods.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.dmesg
documentation:
Prints the GreatFET's debug ring. Convenience function.
To grab the debug ring's contents without printing, see read_debug_ring().

Args:
    max_length -- The maximum length to respond with. Must be less than 65536.
    clear -- True iff the dmesg buffer should be cleared after the request.

class_variables.name: pygreat.board.GreatBoard.HANDLED_BOARD_IDS
documentation:
The display name of the given board. Subclasses should override
this with a more appropriate name.
Class representing a USB-connected GreatFET device.

modules.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb
documentation:
Module containing the definitions necessary to communicate with libgreat
devices over USB.
This file is part of libgreat

classes.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend

methods.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend.__init__
documentation:
Instantiates a new comms connection to a libgreat device; by default connects
to the first available board.

Accepts the same arguments as pyusb's usb.find() method, allowing narrowing
to a more specific board by serial number.
TODO: handle providing board "URIs", like "usb;serial_number=0x123",
and automatic resolution to a backend?

methods.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend._hold_libgreat_interface
documentation:
Ensures we have exclusive access to the USB interface used by libgreat.

This is used internally both to get long-term exclusive access and to
take temporary exclusive access (e.g. during a libgreat command).

parameters:
    timeout -- the maximum amount of time we should wait before

methods.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend._release_libgreat_interface
documentation:
Releases our hold on the USB interface used by libgreat, allowing others
to use it.

Parameters:
    maintain_exclusivity -- if true, we won't release an interface held for
                            long-term exclusive access

methods.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend.get_exclusive_access
documentation:
Take (and hold) exclusive access to the device. Enables optimizations,
as we can make assumptions base on our holding of the device.

methods.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend.release_exclusive_access
documentation: Release exclusive access to the device.

methods.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend._vendor_request
documentation:
Performs a USB vendor-specific control request.

See also _vendor_request_in()/_vendor_request_out(), which provide a
simpler syntax for simple requests.

Args:
    request -- The number of the vendor request to be performed. Usually
        a constant from the protocol.vendor_requests module.
    value -- The value to be passed to the vendor request.

For IN requests:
    length_or_data -- The length of the data expected in response from the request.
For OUT requests:
    length_or_data -- The data to be sent to the device.

methods.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend._vendor_request_in
documentation:
Performs a USB control request that expects a respnose from the GreatFET.

Args:
    request -- The number of the vendor request to be performed. Usually
        a constant from the protocol.vendor_requests module.
    length -- The length of the data expected in response from the request.

methods.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend._vendor_request_in_string
documentation:
Performs a USB control request that expects a respnose from the GreatFET.

Interprets the result as an encoded string.

Args:
    request -- The number of the vendor request to be performed. Usually
        a constant from the protocol.vendor_requests module.
    length -- The length of the data expected in response from the request.

methods.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend._vendor_request_out
documentation:
Performs a USB control request that provides data to the GreatFET.

Args:
    request -- The number of the vendor request to be performed. Usually
        a constant from the protocol.vendor_requests module.
    value -- The value to be passed to the vendor request.

methods.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend._build_command_prelude
documentation:
Builds a libgreat command prelude, which identifies the command
being executed to libgreat.

methods.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend._usb_serial_number
documentation: Reports the device's USB serial number.

methods.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend._check_for_repeat
documentation:
Check to see if the class_number, verb, and data are the same as the immediately
preceeding call to this function. This is used to determine when we can perform a repeat-optimization,
which is documented in execute_raw_command.

methods.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend.execute_raw_command
documentation:
Executes a libgreat command.

Args:
    class_number -- The class number for the given command.
        See the GreatFET wiki for a list of class numbers.
    verb -- The verb number for the given command.
        See the GreatFET wiki for the given class.
    data -- Data to be transmitted to the GreatFET.
    timeout -- Maximum command execution time, in ms.
    encoding -- If specified, the response data will attempt to be
        decoded in the provided format.
    max_response_length -- If less than 4096, this parameter will
        cut off the provided response at the given length.
    comms_timeout -- Maximum execution time for communications that do
        not directly execute the command.
    pretty_name -- String describing the RPC; used for error handling.
    rephrase_errors -- Allow exceptions to be intercepted and rephrased with more details.


Returns any data received in response.

methods.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend.abort_command
documentation:
Aborts execution of a current libgreat command. Used for error handling.

Returns:
    the last error code returned by a command; only meaningful if

methods.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend.close
documentation:
Dispose resources allocated by this connection.  This connection
will no longer be usable.

methods.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend.still_connected
documentation: Attempts to detect if the device is still connected. Returns true iff it is.

class_variables.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend.LIBGREAT_REQUEST_NUMBER
documentation:
Constant value provided to libgreat vendor requests to indicate that the command
should execute normally.

class_variables.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend.LIBGREAT_VALUE_EXECUTE
documentation:
Constant value provided to the libgreat vendor requests indicating that the active
command should be cancelled.

class_variables.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend.LIBGREAT_VALUE_CANCEL
documentation: Constant size of errors returned by libgreat commands on failure.

class_variables.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend.LIBGREAT_ERRNO_SIZE
documentation:
A flag passed to command execution that indicates we exepect no response, and don't need to wait
for anything more than the initial ACK.

class_variables.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb.USBCommsBackend.LIBGREAT_FLAG_SKIP_RESPONSE
documentation:
A flag passed to command execution that indicates that the host should re-use all of the in-arguments
from a previous iteration. See execute_raw_command for documentation.

Class representing an abstract communications channel used to
connect with a libgreat board.

Aborts execution of a current libgreat command. Used for error handling.

Returns:

class_variables.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb1.USB1CommsBackend.LIBGREAT_BULK_OUT_ENDPOINT_NUMBER

class_variables.name: pygreat.comms_backends.usb1.USB1CommsBackend.LIBGREAT_BULK_IN_ENDPOINT_NUMBER

modules.name: pygreat.comms
documentation:
Module containing the general definitions for communicating with
libgreat devices.
This file is part of libgreat

functions.name: pygreat.comms._generate_command_in_signature
documentation:
Generates in-signature documentation for a given RPC.
This acts as input to python's built-in documentation engine.

functions.name: pygreat.comms._generate_command_out_signature
documentation:
Generates out-signature documentation for a given RPC.
This is used to generate a helpful return annotation e.g. for use as
input to python's documentation engine.

functions.name: pygreat.comms._generate_command_rpc_signature
documentation:
Geneates a signature object that describes a generated method call.
This allows our generated method to have standard python docs, if we
want (we do).

functions.name: pygreat.comms.command_rpc
documentation:
Convenience function that creates an RPC method for a given verb.

Args:

: verb_number -- The verb number for the RPC to be created.
  in_format -- The format of the arguments accepted. Defined the same way as CommsBackend.pack.
  out_format -- The format of the return value accepted. Defined the same way as struct.unpack.

This function can be used to quickly define methods that issue libgreat
commands, transparently packing and unpacking arguments to handle the RPC.

For example, in the following code:

```
class ExampleClass(CommsClass):
    read_a_thing = command_rpc(verb_number=1, in_format="<II", out_format="<I")
```

ExampleClass would wind up with a method called `read_a_thing`, which accepts
two arguments and returns a single integer. Arguments will be encoded as two uint32_ts
in the command payload, and the response would be intepreted as containing
a uint32_t.

Calling this method would look like this:

```
x = ExampleClass(comms_backend)
y = x.read_a_thing(1, 2)

# If successful, y should contian an integer!
```

functions.name: pygreat.comms.c_string_arguments
documentation:
Convenience function intended to be passed to in_format which allows
easy arguments which are lists of null-terminated strings.

functions.name: pygreat.comms.c_string_return
documentation:
Convenience function intended to be passed to out_format which allows
easy parsing of return arguments that consist of one or more null-terminated
string.

functions.name: pygreat.comms.int_array_arguments
documentation:
Convenience function that collapses an array of integers into a libgreat
    command payload.

Args:
    specifier -- A single letter specifying the way the integer is to be
                 encoded. Should be one of the format letters that can be
                 provided to struct.pack()

functions.name: pygreat.comms.int_array_return
documentation:
Convenience function that splits a command return into a tuple of
    evenly-spaced arguments.

Args:
    specifier -- A single letter specifying the way the integer is to be
                 encoded. Should be one of the format letters that can be
                 provided to struct.pack()

classes.name: pygreat.comms.CommsError
documentation: Generic class for libgreat communications errors.

classes.name: pygreat.comms.CommandFailureError
documentation: Generic class for command failures.

classes.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend.from_device_uri
documentation: Creates a CommsBackend object appropriate for the given device_uri.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend.__init__
documentation:
Sets up a new communication channel for a libgreat device.

Args:
    device_arguments -- A dictionary of backend-specific arguments; as typically
        parsed from a device URI.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend.generate_api_object
documentation:
Generates an object that gives us a view of all API methods
accessible via communciations API. Identical to touching the .apis
dictionary, but uses object semantics.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend.run_autoenumeration
documentation:
Uses the Core Introspection API to determine the accessible APIs
and automatically genereate RPC stubs in the .api accessor.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._generate_object_for_class
documentation:
Uses the Core Introspection API to generate a python class containing
    RPCs for the provided libgreat class.

Args:
    class_number -- The class number to generate RPCs for.
    overwrite -- If true, existing class definitions will be overwritten.
        Otherwise, any class that already has an object associated with it
        will be maintained.

Returns:
    a dynamically-generated class object for the provieded class number,
    filled with RPC methods

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._parse_rpc_param_names_string
documentation: Parses a comma-separated command string into a list of names.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._generate_rpc_verbs_for_class
documentation:
Uses the Core Introspection API to generate RPCs for each of the verbs
    supported by a given class.
Args:
    class_number -- The class number to generate RPCs for.

Returns:
    a dictionary mapping verb names to yet-unbound method objects

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._split_struct_string
documentation:
Splits a libgreat pack/unpack format string into groups
that can be passed to a subordinate handler function.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._is_special_format
documentation:
Returns true iff the given format string represents one of
our extensions to struct.pack.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._format_string_argument_count
documentation:
Returns the number of arguments that will be consumed by
the given format string. Format strings must not contain '*'.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._split_off_arguments_for_format
documentation:
Splits off the number of arguments necessary to handle the
given format string.

Returns:
    a 2-tuple of args_consumed, args_remaining

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._get_bytes_consumed_by_group_format
documentation:
Returns the number of bytes consumed by a given grouped
format string. Accepts format strings that do not begin with '*'.

Intended for use by _get_bytes_consumed_by_format.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._get_bytes_consumed_by_format
documentation:
Returns the number of bytes consumed by a given
format string.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._split_off_bytes_for_format
documentation:
Splits off the bytes necessary to handle the
given format string.

Returns:
    a 2-tuple of bytes_consumed, bytes_remaining

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._pack_group
documentation:
Handles packing of a group subformat, which groups
a pack string using parenthesis.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._unpack_group
documentation:
Handles packing of a group subformat, which groups
a pack string using parenthesis.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend.pack
documentation:
Extended version of struct.pack() for libgreat communciations.

Accepts mostly the same arguments as struct.pack, with the following
additional supported format strings:

    S -- c-style, UTF-8, null-terminated string
    X -- as-is; returns a byte-string. If specified with a
         repeat specifier, each of the bytes will be squished
         together into a single string.

Adds the following additional prefixes:

    * -- acts likes a repeat prefix, but consumes all remaining arguments
         as though they were the given type; so the format "*B" would
         pack all remaining arguments into uint8_ts

Also accepts grouped arguments, which are grouped by parentheses,
and accept indexable collections, like tuples. So, for example:

    (BH) -- would accept a tuple of a uint8 and a uint16

Grouped arguments are especially useful with repeat count 'prefixes'.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._unpack
documentation:
Extended version of struct.unpack() for libgreat communciations.

Accepts mostly the same arguments as struct.pack, with the following
additional supported format strings:

    S -- c-style, UTF-8, null-terminated string
    X -- as-is; returns a byte-string. If specified with a
         repeat specifier, each of the bytes will be squished
         together into a single string.

Adds the following additional prefixes:

    * -- acts likes a repeat prefix, but consumes all remaining arguments
         as though they were the given type; so the format "*B" would
         unpack all remaining bytes into uint8's

Also accepts grouped arguments, which are grouped by parentheses,
and unpack into tuples. So, for example:

    (BH) -- would parse three bytes into a tuple of a uint8 and a uint16

Grouped arguments are especially useful with repeat count 'prefixes'.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend.argument_annotations_for_format
documentation:
Returns a list of strings that describe each argument of a format.
Intended to be used to generate argument annotations.

Args:
    format_string -- The format to be described.

Returns:
    a list of strings describing each argument

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend.execute_command
documentation:
Executes a libgreat command.

Args:
    class_number -- The class number for the given command.
        See the GreatFET wiki for a list of class numbers.
    verb -- The verb number for the given command.
        See the GreatFET wiki for the given class.

    in_format -- Specifies the input format of the arguments,
        in one of the formats specified below.,
    out_format -- Specifies the format in which the output argumnt
        will be parsed; in one of the formats specified below.
    *arguments -- The positional arguments to be passed to the libgreat
        device. These work as if passed to struct.pack.

    timeout -- Maximum command execution time, in ms.
    encoding -- If specified, any strings in response data will be
        decoded in the provided format.
    max_response_length -- If less than 4096, this parameter will
        cut off the provided response at the given length.
    name -- name of the command being executed; for error messages
        only
    class_name -- name of the class the command belongs to; for error messages only
    rephase_errors -- true if we should be allowed to rephrase errors that happen in the backend
        to add more detail

The formats used by in_format and out_format can be as follows:
    - A format string in the format accepted by struct.pack;
    - A format string in the format accepted by CommsBackend.pack;
    - A callable object, which handles the conversion between tuple
      and byte payload itself.

Returns any data recieved in response, parsed according to out_format.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._command_results_should_collapse
documentation:
Returns true if the given command result should be collapsed
into a single value.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend.execute_raw_command
documentation:
Executes a libgreat command.

Args:
    class_number -- The class number for the given command.
        See the GreatFET wiki for a list of class numbers.
    verb -- The verb number for the given command.
        See the GreatFET wiki for the given class.
    data -- Data to be transmitted to the GreatFET.
    timeout -- Maximum command execution time, in ms.
    encoding -- If specified, the response data will attempt to be
        decoded in the provided format.
    max_response_length -- If less than 4096, this parameter will
        cut off the provided response at the given length.
    pretty_name -- String describing the RPC; used for error handling.
    rephrase_errors -- Allow exceptions to be intercepted and rephrased with more details.

Returns any data recieved in response.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._strip_dmesg_timestamp
documentation: Removes any timestamp prefix from a dmesg line.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._try_to_generate_error_context
documentation: Attempts to generate a string providing error context.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._exception_for_command_failure
documentation:
Generates an CommandFailureError for a failed command.

Arguments:
    error_number -- The libgreat error number returned during command failure.
    pretty_name -- The pretty name of the RPC that failed, usually in class.name format.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend.get_exclusive_access
documentation:
When possible, attempts to ensure exclusive access to the device.

In some backends, this enables optimizations.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend.release_exclusive_access
documentation: Releases any exclusive access we hold.

class_variables.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend.LIBGREAT_MAX_COMMAND_SIZE
documentation: Regular expression that identifies special fields for .pack and .unpack.

class_variables.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._SPECIAL_FIELD_REGEX
documentation: Regular expression that splits the parts of a .pack / .unpack format expression.

class_variables.name: pygreat.comms.CommsBackend._FORMAT_FIELD_REGEX
documentation: Dictionary that describes nice names for each of the format fields.

Class representing an abstract communications channel used to
connect with a libgreat board.

classes.name: pygreat.comms.CommsApiCollection

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsApiCollection.__getattr__
documentation: Trivial definition of __getattr_. Makes linters happy.

Dynamically-allocated container object that is automatically
populated with API objects. Provides a view of our dictionary
of APIs that has object semantics.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsClass.__init__

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsClass.supports_verb
documentation: Returns true iff the given comms class supports a verb with the given name.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsClass.execute_command
documentation:
Executes a libgreat command.

Args:
    verb -- The verb number for the given command.
        See the GreatFET wiki for the given class.

    in_format -- Specifies the input format of the arguments,
        in the same format as used by struct.pack.
    out_format -- Specifies the format in which the output argumnt
        will be parsed; in the same format used by struct.unpack.
    *arguments -- The positional arguments to be passed to the libgreat
        device. These work as if passed to struct.pack.

    timeout -- Maximum command execution time, in ms.
    encoding -- If specified, any strings in response data will be
        decoded in the provided format.
    max_response_length -- If less than 4096, this parameter will
        cut off the provided response at the given length.

Returns any data recieved in response, parsed according to out_format.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsClass.execute_raw_command
documentation:
Executes a libgreat command.

Args:
    class_number -- The class number for the given command.
        See the GreatFET wiki for a list of class numbers.
    verb -- The verb number for the given command.
        See the GreatFET wiki for the given class.
    data -- Data to be transmitted to the GreatFET.
    timeout -- Maximum command execution time, in ms.
    encoding -- If specified, the response data will attempt to be
        decoded in the provided format.
    max_response_length -- If less than 4096, this parameter will
        cut off the provided response at the given length.
    pretty_name -- String describing the RPC; used for error handling.
    rephrase_errors -- Allow exceptions to be intercepted and rephrased with more details.

Returns any data recieved in response.

methods.name: pygreat.comms.CommsClass.still_connected
documentation: Attempts to detect if the device is still connected. Returns true iff it is.

Class representing an libgreat communications class, which exposes
a set of functionality to the host. Classes group _verbs_, which act
like remote procedure calls.

classes.name: pygreat.comms.GeneratedCommsClass
documentation:
Special variant of CommsClass used as the parent of procedurally-
genereated CommsClass. Can be used to differentiate automatically and
manually generated classes.
